commit.message:
fix(v0.3.20): corregir 4 bugs críticos introducidos en v0.3.17

Bugs corregidos (todos introducidos entre v0.3.16 → v0.3.17):

1. SQLite schema: CREATE TABLE de links no incluía is_read/notes/reminder_at
   aunque schema.sqlite.ts sí los tenía → TODOS los endpoints que tocaban
   la tabla links devolvían 500 "no such column" en modo desktop.
   Fix: sqlite-driver.ts actualizado + runSQLiteMigrations() con los 3 campos.

2. ThemeProvider: los 11 temas nuevos no estaban en el prop themes=[] de
   Providers.tsx → next-themes no los gestionaba → seleccionar nordic,
   catppuccin, tokyo, etc. no aplicaba ningún estilo.
   Fix: Providers.tsx actualizado con los 24 temas.

3. SSR crash: useElectron.ts accedía a window.isElectron sin guard en
   el initializer de useState → "window is not defined" durante SSR.
   Fix: typeof window !== "undefined" añadido.

4. Pantalla negra en tauri:dev: lib.rs en modo #[cfg(dev)] no navegaba
   al devUrl antes de show() → ventana quedaba en about:blank.
   Fix: hilo separado navega a http://localhost:7878 con 600ms de retardo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace silent except:pass with real error logging in Miel I/O

The Miel persistence layer was silently swallowing all exceptions during
save/load operations (disk full, permission denied, corrupted JSON).
Now logs specific errors to stderr: PermissionError, OSError,
JSONDecodeError, TypeError/ValueError — the honey no longer dies in silence.

AI security audit round 3: OWASP LLM Top 10 + EU AI Act fixes

CRITICAL (OWASP LLM01): Prompt injection sanitizer — strip XML tags,
  injection patterns, code blocks from student input
CRITICAL (OWASP LLM02): Output content filter — block unsafe patterns
  in LLM responses (child safety)
HIGH (OWASP LLM09): LLM fallback now signals FALLBACK status instead
  of silently degrading as IN_SCOPE
MEDIUM (EU AI Act Art. 52): AI disclosure banner in chat panel
MEDIUM (OWASP LLM06): Strip internal metadata (document IDs, scores,
  chunk counts) from client responses
MEDIUM (OWASP LLM05): Fail fast if OpenRouter API key is missing
LOW: Rate limiter warns in production when falling back to in-memory

Fix Vercel build: safe Supabase client init during prerender

createBrowserClient throws when env vars are undefined during static
prerendering. Added fallback values so the client can be constructed
safely — it is never actually used during prerender since all
Supabase calls run inside useEffect/callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent double-delete on Tile when slide and tap gestures race

Tile.delete() now checks isDeleting before proceeding. The slide gesture
sets isDeleting=true during its animation, but a tap gesture could fire
on the same tile before the animation completes, calling delete() again
after the node was already removed from its parent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove rejection penalty from IS log-likelihood estimator

Rejected trees (hitting max_lambda or max_missing limits) are
computational failures, not zero-likelihood samples from the proposal.
They should simply be discarded, not penalised via log(n_valid/n_total).

Removed the n_rejected parameter from .is_fhat() and .bootstrap_fhat_var().
The rejection count is still tracked in best_IS$n_rejected for diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): electron-builder publish to non-draft release (#37)

electron-builder defaults to creating draft releases, but the daemon
workflow creates a non-draft release first. This caused electron-builder
to skip uploading desktop assets (dmg, exe, AppImage) with the error:
"existing type not compatible with publishing type".

Adding releaseType: "release" makes both workflows compatible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: Parallelize aggregation data loading with asyncio.gather

Previously, source boards and advisor history were loaded sequentially
(each with its own asyncio.run call). Now everything runs concurrently
in a single event loop:
- All source boards load in parallel
- Advisor history groups also load in parallel
- Advisor history runs concurrently with source boards

This should significantly reduce loading time, especially when there
are multiple source boards and many months of advisor history.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert _repl construction in __init__ back to type annotations

Now that tools are fresh per-request (not singletons), the band-aid
from 128f841 is unnecessary. Restore the original init/close lifecycle:
__init__ declares the type, init() creates the instance, close() deletes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Module 5 — Adzuna job search integration (384 tests passing) (#6)

* fix(ci): resolve cryptography build failure in CI pipeline

* fix(i18n): add lang_* keys for all 39 languages

* feat(ui): Module 2 — virtual on-screen keyboard (7 scripts)

- Add app/ui/virtual_keyboard.py with KEYBOARD_SCRIPTS (39 langs), KEYBOARD_LAYOUTS
  (7 scripts: arabic, nastaliq, devanagari, bengali, tamil, ethiopic, somali_latin),
  and public API: get_script_for_language(), needs_virtual_keyboard(),
  get_keyboard_output(), clear_keyboard_output(), render_virtual_keyboard()
- Add app/tests/test_virtual_keyboard.py — 55 tests across 5 classes, all passing
- Integrate render_virtual_keyboard("pi_summary", lang) into cv_builder.py after
  the professional summary text_area
- Add 6 new i18n keys (kb_backspace, kb_space, kb_clear, kb_dismiss,
  kb_show_keyboard, kb_hide_keyboard) with native-language translations to all
  39 language files (211 keys per file)

* fix(deps): rebuild requirements.txt for Linux CI compatibility

Removed 4 packages not imported anywhere in the codebase:
- appscript==1.4.0: macOS-only (Apple Script), no Linux binary wheel,
  fails to build from source on Linux (gcc error). Root cause of CI failure.
- xlwings==0.33.20: Windows/macOS Excel desktop automation, not a server
  dependency, not imported anywhere.
- pyroaring==1.0.3: not imported anywhere in the codebase.
- pyiceberg==0.11.0: heavyweight data lake library, not imported anywhere.

Updated 1 version pin:
- streamlit==1.54.0 -> 1.55.0 (latest stable; 1.55.0 is what installs
  on current Linux environment).

All 217 runnable tests still pass after these changes.

* feat(ui): Module 3 — RTL layout support (7 languages)

- Add app/ui/rtl_support.py with three public functions:
    apply_rtl_styles(lang): injects RTL CSS (html/body, inputs, textareas,
      selects, .stMarkdown, .stForm, flex row-reverse for columns, sidebar
      right-positioned, buttons right-aligned) via st.markdown when
      is_rtl(lang) is True; no-op for LTR languages.
    get_rtl_column_order(cols): reverses column list for RTL session language.
    rtl_text(text, lang): wraps text in direction:rtl span for RTL; returns
      plain text for LTR.
- Add app/tests/test_rtl_support.py — 59 tests across 4 classes, all passing.
- Integrate apply_rtl_styles(lang) into all 6 UI entry points that already
  call get_language(): cv_builder, ats_page, admin_page, coach_page,
  login_page, app.py.
- Add rtl flag to cv_service.export_cv() signature (bool, default False).
  RTL PDF rendering deferred to Module 15.

RTL languages covered: ar, ur, ps, fa, ku, he, sd.
All 276 tests pass (217 existing + 59 new).

* feat(security): Module 4 — hCaptcha integration

- Add app/security/captcha.py:
    verify_hcaptcha(token, secret): POSTs to hCaptcha siteverify API,
      returns True on success, False on failure/exception/empty args.
      Never raises. Handles network errors, timeouts, malformed responses.
    is_captcha_required(role): returns False for SystemAdmin (via
      has_permission() check on sysadmin.panel.access), True for all
      other roles and unauthenticated users (None).
    get_hcaptcha_site_key(): reads HCAPTCHA_SITE_KEY env var.
    get_hcaptcha_secret(): reads HCAPTCHA_SECRET env var.

- Add app/ui/captcha_widget.py:
    render_captcha_placeholder(lang): st.info banner + st.text_input
      for token paste. All labels via t(). Streamlit-phase implementation;
      full browser widget in Module 15 (React frontend).
    get_captcha_token_from_session(): returns token from session state.
    clear_captcha_token(): resets token after use.

- Modify app/services/auth_service.py: add captcha_token and captcha_role
  params to register() and login(). CAPTCHA enforced when HCAPTCHA_SECRET
  is set; SystemAdmin bypasses; raises AuthError("captcha_invalid") on
  failure.

- Add 5 captcha_* i18n keys to all 39 language files (216 keys per file):
  captcha_required, captcha_token_label, captcha_token_help,
  captcha_invalid, captcha_bypass_admin.

- Add HCAPTCHA_SITE_KEY and HCAPTCHA_SECRET placeholder entries to .env
  and .env.save.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support leading doc comments (///) on state: declarations (BT-1159) (#1208)

## Summary

Activates support for `///` doc comments on `state:` declarations — the
parser already captured them but the formatter rendered them with broken
indentation.

- Removes the stale "Always `None` until BT-975" note from
`StateDeclaration.doc_comment` in `ast.rs` — the field has been
populated by the parser since BT-975 landed
- Fixes `unparse_class_definition` to wrap state/class-variable
declarations in `nest(2, ...)`, matching the pattern used for methods,
so that doc-comment lines and leading `//` comments inside a state
declaration are correctly indented at column 2
- Adds three unparse unit tests: single-line doc comment, multi-line doc
comment, and full parse→unparse round-trip

## Example (now working)

```beamtalk
Actor subclass: HTTPClient
  /// Base URL prepended to all relative paths.
  state: baseUrl = ""

  /// Request timeout in milliseconds.
  state: timeout = 30000
```

## Key Changes

- `crates/beamtalk-core/src/ast.rs` — remove stale BT-975 note
- `crates/beamtalk-core/src/unparse/mod.rs` — use `nest(2, ...)` for
state declarations; add tests

https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1159

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
* Added tests to ensure documentation comments on state declarations are
preserved and round-tripped.

* **Style**
* Standardized 2-space indentation and alignment for state and class
variable declarations so doc-comments render consistently.

* **Documentation**
* Converted and expanded inline notes into block-style doc comments for
public response fields (status, headers, body).

commit.message:
feat: add GLB furniture loading with useNativeGLTF and Suspense fallback

Step 3: Replace box placeholders with GLB models for furniture items that
have a modelId → URL mapping. Uses useNativeGLTF (triggers Suspense) with
box placeholder as fallback while loading. Materials overridden with flat
MeshStandardMaterial colors (PBR textures don't work on expo-gl).
Selection highlight via emissive on GLB meshes. Two test models mapped:
coffee_table → BoxTextured.glb, armchair → Duck.glb from Khronos samples.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: r3f-native-testbed
repository.description: Expo testbed app for @react-three/native

commit.message:
Add touch swipe gesture support

Detect touchstart/touchend on the swipe card and trigger left/right
swipe if the horizontal distance exceeds 50px.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace dashboard spinner with skeleton UI; fix Vercel Blob token error

- Dashboard loading state shows skeleton cards (welcome banner, action grid,
  activity rows) instead of a blocking centered spinner
- Upload route: move auth check into onBeforeGenerateToken only, so Vercel
  CDN completion webhooks (no user cookies) are not blocked by a top-level 401

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prioritize Speed to Lead, lead-to-appt ratio, and follow-ups in portal

- Speed to Lead grade (A/B/C/D) is now the FIRST thing clients see
- Shows avg response time, % called in 5 min, % leads called, uncalled count
- Each metric has a goal indicator (green/amber/red)
- Lead-to-Appt ratio for both seller and buyer shown prominently
- Avg follow-ups per lead metric added
- Ad performance KPIs moved below accountability metrics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add Security Radar rule generation workflow

- New docs/security-radar-rule-generation.md with rule formats for all
  7 config targets (injection, egress, code enforcer, supply chain,
  output sanitizer, image safety, skill scanner)
- Generic workflow: classify risk → draft rule → present for approval → apply
- Reference from CLAUDE.md agent delegation section
- Uses ~/.vex-talon/config/ paths (plugin-standard)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: vex-talon
repository.description: 20-layer defense-in-depth security plugin for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix file name overflow in activity modal with overflow-hidden

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add apis.google.com to CSP script-src for Firebase Google sign-in

The CSP was blocking https://apis.google.com/js/api.js which Firebase
Auth requires for the Google sign-in popup flow.
